actors.name: User
actors.description: General user interacting with Berachain dApps, wallets, and participating in the ecosystem. (doc_5, doc_6, doc_11, doc_12, doc_13)

actors.name: Validator
actors.description: Participant in Proof-of-Liquidity responsible for staking $BERA, proposing blocks, directing $BGT emissions, and earning incentives. (doc_7, doc_20, doc_21, doc_24)

actors.name: $BGT Holder
actors.description: Holder of $BGT tokens, participating in governance by voting, delegating $BGT to validators (boosting), and earning incentives. (doc_3, doc_7, doc_24, doc_27)

actors.name: Liquidity Provider
actors.description: User who supplies liquidity to pools (e.g., on BEX) and earns rewards, including $BGT by staking LP tokens in Reward Vaults. (doc_7, doc_11, doc_24)
capabilities: Stake LP tokens in Reward Vaults

actors.name: Protocol (Ecosystem Project)
actors.description: An entity, group, or organization that deploys dApps and Reward Vaults on Berachain, offering incentives to attract $BGT emissions and users. (doc_7, doc_14, doc_21, doc_24)
capabilities: Offer Incentive Tokens in Reward Vaults

actors.name: Guardian
actors.description: A 5-of-9 multisig with the power to cancel malicious governance proposals during the timelock period. (doc_3, doc_14)

actors.name: Token Manager
actors.description: A wallet address responsible for managing and offering Incentive Tokens for a specific Reward Vault. (doc_15, doc_21)
capabilities: Add Incentive Tokens to Reward Vault

actors.name: Airdrop Recipient
actors.description: User eligible for the $BERA airdrop, including testnet users, NFT holders, and other community members. (doc_2, doc_29)

actors.name: NFT Holder
actors.description: Owner of official Berachain NFTs, potentially eligible for airdrops and able to bridge NFTs between Ethereum and Berachain. (doc_2, doc_4, doc_29)

actors.name: Initial Core Contributors
actors.description: Members of Big Bera Labs and advisors who contributed to the development of Berachain and receive a token allocation. (doc_29)
capabilities: Receive $BERA token allocation subject to vesting

actors.name: Investors
actors.description: Seed, Series A, and Series B investors in Berachain who receive a token allocation. (doc_29)
capabilities: Receive $BERA token allocation subject to vesting

actors.name: BGT Foundation
actors.description: Entity responsible for reviewing Request for Reward Vault (RFRV) proposals. (doc_14)

assets.name: $BERA
assets.description: Native gas token for transactions and staking token for validators on Berachain. (doc_6, doc_26, doc_29)

assets.name: $BGT (Bera Governance Token)
assets.description: Non-transferrable token for governance participation (voting) and influencing economic incentives (boosting validators). Earned through PoL. (doc_3, doc_7, doc_27)

assets.name: $HONEY
assets.description: Native, fully collateralized stablecoin of the Berachain ecosystem, soft-pegged to the US Dollar. (doc_12, doc_18, doc_28)

assets.name: Official Berachain NFTs
assets.description: Collections like Bong Bears, Bond Bears, etc., which can be bridged and may confer benefits like airdrops. (doc_4)

assets.name: LP Tokens
assets.description: Receipt tokens representing a user's share in a liquidity pool (e.g., in BEX). Can be staked in Reward Vaults. (doc_11, doc_18)

assets.name: PoL-eligible receipt tokens
assets.description: General class of tokens received for performing productive actions, which can be staked in Reward Vaults to earn $BGT. (doc_7, doc_24, doc_25)

assets.name: Incentive Tokens
assets.description: Whitelisted ERC20 tokens offered by protocols within their Reward Vaults to attract $BGT emissions from validators. (doc_15, doc_21)

assets.name: Governance Proposals
assets.description: On-chain proposals created by $BGT holders for making decisions about the Berachain ecosystem and its core dApps. (doc_3)

assets.name: Private Keys
assets.description: Cryptographic keys stored in user wallets, granting access to and control over their on-chain assets. (doc_5)

assets.name: User Address
assets.description: A public blockchain address associated with a user's wallet, used for transactions and identification (e.g., for airdrop checking). (doc_2)

assets.name: Social Connections
assets.description: User's social media account connections used for verifying eligibility for certain airdrop allocations. (doc_2)

assets.name: Whitelisted Collateral for $HONEY
assets.description: Approved stablecoins or assets (e.g., $USDC, $BYUSD) that can be deposited to mint $HONEY. (doc_28)

components.name: Berachain
components.description: High-performance, EVM-Identical Layer 1 blockchain utilizing Proof-of-Liquidity. (doc_9)

components.name: BeaconKit
components.description: Modular and customizable consensus layer framework for Ethereum-based blockchains, used by Berachain. (doc_8, doc_9)

components.name: EVM (Ethereum Virtual Machine)
components.description: The execution environment for smart contracts on Berachain, which is EVM-identical. (doc_9)

components.name: CometBFT
components.description: Consensus algorithm leveraged by BeaconKit to achieve fast finality. (doc_8, doc_19)

components.name: Execution Clients
components.description: Software (e.g., Geth, Reth) that processes transactions and executes smart contracts within the EVM. (doc_9, doc_19)

components.name: BEX (Berachain Exchange)
components.description: Berachain's native decentralized exchange (DEX) for trading assets and providing liquidity. (doc_11, doc_13)

components.name: Honey Swap
components.description: Native dApp allowing users to trade approved stables and assets for $HONEY. (doc_12, doc_28)

components.name: BeraHub
components.description: Central dApp for managing $BGT, accessing BEX, participating in governance, and claiming rewards. (doc_13)

components.name: Reward Vaults
components.description: Smart contracts where users stake PoL-eligible assets (e.g., LP tokens) to earn $BGT emissions. (doc_7, doc_14, doc_25)

components.name: Berachain Bridge
components.description: Canonical bridge, powered by LayerZero, for transferring tokens between Berachain and other blockchains. (doc_6)

components.name: Berachain NFT Bridge
components.description: Bridge for transferring official Berachain NFTs between Ethereum and Berachain. (doc_4)

components.name: Governance Contracts
components.description: Smart contracts, based on OpenZeppelin's framework, that manage the lifecycle of governance proposals. (doc_3)

components.name: Timelock Contract
components.description: A smart contract that enforces a delay before a successful governance proposal can be executed. (doc_3)

components.name: Distributor Contract
components.description: Smart contract responsible for distributing $BGT emissions to whitelisted Reward Vaults. (doc_20)

components.name: BGTIncentiveDistributor Contract
components.description: Smart contract that manages the distribution and claiming of Incentive Tokens for users who have boosted validators. (doc_21)

components.name: FeeCollector Contract
components.description: Smart contract that collects fees from core dApps (BEX, HoneySwap), auctions them for $WBERA, and distributes proceeds to $BGT boosters. (doc_27)

components.name: HoneyFactory Contract
components.description: Central smart contract that manages the minting and burning of $HONEY by interacting with specific collateral vaults. (doc_28)

components.name: $HONEY Vaults
components.description: Specialized smart contracts, one for each type of whitelisted collateral, used in the $HONEY minting/burning process. (doc_28)

components.name: Wallets
components.description: User-controlled software (e.g., MetaMask, Rabby) for managing private keys and interacting with Berachain. (doc_5)

components.name: Berachain RPC Endpoints
components.description: Network endpoints (Mainnet and Bepolia Testnet) that wallets and applications use to connect to the Berachain network. (doc_5)

components.name: Airdrop Checker Tool
components.description: A website (checker.berachain.com) allowing users to check their $BERA airdrop allocation. (doc_2)

components.name: Airdrop Claim Tool
components.description: A website (airdrop.berachain.com) allowing eligible users to claim their $BERA airdrop. (doc_2)

components.name: Governance Forum
components.description: Online forum (hub.forum.berachain.com) for discussing governance proposals, including RFRV submissions. (doc_14)

components.name: BlockRewardController Contract
components.description: On-chain smart contract storing constants related to block reward emissions. (doc_22)

components.name: Auto-Incentivizer Mechanism
components.description: System where fees from foundation vaults are used to automatically offer incentives. (doc_22)

data_flows.data: RPC requests (read/write blockchain state, send transactions)
data_flows.description: User connects wallet to Berachain network via RPC. (doc_5)

data_flows.data: Tokens from external chain / $BERA to user wallet
data_flows.description: User obtains $BERA via bridging. (doc_6)
Validator stakes $BERA to participate in consensus. (doc_7, doc_26)
Block production results in $BGT emission to proposing validator. (doc_7, doc_20)
Validator directs $BGT emissions to Reward Vaults and receives protocol-provided Incentives. (doc_7, doc_21)
User (Liquidity Provider) provides liquidity, receives LP tokens, stakes LP tokens in Reward Vault, earns $BGT. (doc_7, doc_11, doc_27)
User ($BGT Holder) delegates (boosts) $BGT to a validator, influencing validator's $BGT emissions and earning a share of incentives. (doc_7, doc_16, doc_21, doc_27)
Governance proposal lifecycle (creation, pending, voting, outcome, timelock, execution). (doc_3)
Guardian oversight and proposal cancellation during timelock. (doc_3)
User (Airdrop Recipient) checks $BERA airdrop allocation and claims tokens. (doc_2)

data_flows.data: NFTs from Ethereum, NFTs to Berachain wallet
data_flows.description: User (NFT Holder) bridges NFTs from Ethereum to Berachain via NFT Bridge. (doc_4)

data_flows.data: Token A for swap, Token B received
data_flows.description: User swaps tokens on BEX. (doc_11)
User trades assets for $HONEY on Honey Swap. (doc_12)
User ($BGT Holder) redeems $BGT for $BERA via BeraHub. (doc_13, doc_27)

data_flows.data: Request for Reward Vault (RFRV) form and forum post
data_flows.description: Protocol submits Request for Reward Vault (RFRV) for whitelisting. (doc_14)
Token Manager adds Incentive Tokens to a Reward Vault. (doc_15, doc_21)
User ($BGT Holder) claims earned Incentives from boosted validators via BeraHub (interacting with BGTIncentiveDistributor). (doc_17, doc_21)
User mints $HONEY by depositing whitelisted collateral into HoneyFactory. (doc_28)
$HONEY redemption, including Basket Mode during depegging. (doc_28)
Fees from $HONEY minting/redemption distributed to $BGT holders (via FeeCollector). (doc_28, doc_27)
Fees from core dApps (BEX, HoneySwap) collected by FeeCollector and distributed to $BGT boosters. (doc_27)

trust_boundaries.name: User Wallet <-> dApp Interface
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between the user's wallet software and the web interface of a dApp. (doc_5, doc_11, doc_12, doc_13)

trust_boundaries.name: dApp Interface <-> Berachain Smart Contracts
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between the dApp's frontend/backend and the on-chain smart contracts on Berachain. (doc_11, doc_25)

trust_boundaries.name: Berachain Network <-> External Chains (via Bridge)
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary where assets are transferred between Berachain and other blockchains like Ethereum. (doc_4, doc_6)

trust_boundaries.name: User <-> Centralized Exchange (CEX)
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary when a user interacts with a CEX to acquire $BERA. The CEX is an external, trusted entity. (doc_6)

trust_boundaries.name: User <-> Airdrop Infrastructure
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary between the user and the web tools (Checker/Claim) for the airdrop process. (doc_2)

trust_boundaries.name: Governance Participants <-> Governance Smart Contracts
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary for submitting proposals and casting votes via governance contracts. (doc_3)

trust_boundaries.name: Guardians (Multisig) <-> Timelock Contract
trust_boundaries.description: Boundary for Guardians to interact with the Timelock contract for emergency actions. (doc_3)

behaviors.name: Connect Wallet to Berachain
behaviors.action: Establish connection via RPC
behaviors.description: User connects their wallet (e.g., MetaMask) to the Berachain network using RPC details. (doc_5)

behaviors.name: Get $BERA
behaviors.action: Bridge tokens or purchase from CEX
behaviors.description: User acquires $BERA either by bridging assets from another chain or buying from a Centralized Exchange. (doc_6)

behaviors.name: Stake $BERA
behaviors.action: Lock $BERA to become a validator
behaviors.description: A validator stakes $BERA to participate in network consensus and block production. (doc_7, doc_26)

behaviors.name: Propose Block
behaviors.action: Create and broadcast a new block
behaviors.description: An active validator proposes a new block of transactions to the network. (doc_7, doc_20)

behaviors.name: Direct $BGT Emissions
behaviors.action: Allocate $BGT rewards to selected Reward Vaults
behaviors.description: Validator chooses which Reward Vaults receive a portion of their earned $BGT emissions. (doc_7, doc_20, doc_21)

behaviors.name: Provide Liquidity
behaviors.action: Deposit assets into a liquidity pool
behaviors.description: A liquidity provider adds tokens to a BEX pool (or similar) and receives LP tokens. (doc_7, doc_11)

behaviors.name: Stake PoL-eligible Receipt Tokens
behaviors.action: Deposit receipt tokens (e.g., LP tokens) into a Reward Vault
behaviors.description: User stakes their PoL-eligible receipt tokens to become eligible for $BGT rewards. (doc_7, doc_25, doc_27)

behaviors.name: Earn $BGT from Reward Vaults
behaviors.action: Distribute $BGT rewards to stakers
behaviors.description: Users who have staked in Reward Vaults accumulate $BGT rewards over time. (doc_7, doc_25, doc_27)

behaviors.name: Delegate $BGT to Validator (Boost)
behaviors.action: Associate $BGT with a chosen validator
behaviors.description: A $BGT holder delegates their $BGT to a validator to increase the validator's boost and share in incentives. (doc_7, doc_13, doc_16, doc_27)

behaviors.name: Claim Incentives
behaviors.action: Withdraw earned incentive tokens
behaviors.description: A $BGT holder who has boosted a validator claims their share of the incentive tokens earned by that validator. (doc_13, doc_17, doc_21)

behaviors.name: Create Governance Proposal
behaviors.action: Submit a new proposal for on-chain governance
behaviors.description: A $BGT holder with sufficient voting power creates a new governance proposal. (doc_3, doc_13)

behaviors.name: Vote on Governance Proposal
behaviors.action: Cast a vote (for, against, abstain) on an active proposal
behaviors.description: $BGT holders participate in decision-making by voting on active governance proposals. (doc_3, doc_27)

behaviors.name: Execute Governance Proposal
behaviors.action: Trigger the execution of a passed and timelocked proposal
behaviors.description: After a proposal succeeds and its timelock period ends, any user can trigger its execution. (doc_3)

behaviors.name: Cancel Malicious Proposal
behaviors.action: Cancel a proposal deemed malicious during its timelock period
behaviors.description: Guardians, via multisig, can intervene to cancel harmful proposals. (doc_3)

behaviors.name: Check $BERA Airdrop Allocation
behaviors.action: Query airdrop eligibility and amount
behaviors.description: User checks their potential $BERA airdrop allocation using the official checker tool. (doc_2)

behaviors.name: Claim $BERA Airdrop
behaviors.action: Execute transaction to receive airdropped $BERA
behaviors.description: Eligible user claims their allocated $BERA tokens through the official claim tool. (doc_2)

behaviors.name: Bridge NFTs
behaviors.action: Transfer official Berachain NFTs between Ethereum and Berachain
behaviors.description: NFT holder uses the Berachain NFT Bridge to move their NFTs. (doc_4)

behaviors.name: Swap Tokens on BEX
behaviors.action: Exchange one cryptocurrency for another
behaviors.description: User trades tokens using the Berachain Exchange (BEX). (doc_11, doc_13)

behaviors.name: Trade for $HONEY on Honey Swap
behaviors.action: Exchange approved assets for $HONEY stablecoin
behaviors.description: User acquires $HONEY by trading other assets on the Honey Swap dApp. (doc_12)

behaviors.name: Redeem $BGT for $BERA
behaviors.action: Burn $BGT to receive an equivalent amount of $BERA
behaviors.description: $BGT holder converts their $BGT into $BERA via BeraHub (one-way). (doc_13, doc_27)

behaviors.name: Claim $BGT Rewards
behaviors.action: Withdraw accumulated $BGT from Reward Vaults
behaviors.description: User who staked in Reward Vaults claims their earned $BGT via BeraHub. (doc_13, doc_27)

behaviors.name: Submit Request for Reward Vault (RFRV)
behaviors.action: Post RFRV form and details on Governance Forum
behaviors.description: A protocol submits an RFRV to get their Reward Vault considered for whitelisting. (doc_14)

behaviors.name: Add Incentives to Reward Vault
behaviors.action: Deposit Incentive Tokens and set rate for a Reward Vault
behaviors.description: The Token Manager for a Reward Vault supplies Incentive Tokens to attract $BGT emissions. (doc_15, doc_21)

behaviors.name: Mint $HONEY
behaviors.action: Deposit whitelisted collateral to create new $HONEY
behaviors.description: User deposits approved collateral (e.g., $USDC) into the HoneyFactory to mint $HONEY. (doc_28)

behaviors.name: Redeem $HONEY
behaviors.action: Burn $HONEY to receive underlying collateral
behaviors.description: User burns $HONEY through the HoneyFactory to get back their deposited collateral. (doc_28)

behaviors.name: Pay Transaction Fees
behaviors.action: Expend $BERA to cover gas costs for a transaction
behaviors.description: Users pay transaction fees in $BERA for any on-chain action. (doc_26)

behaviors.name: Create Reward Vault
behaviors.action: Deploy a new Reward Vault smart contract
behaviors.description: Protocols can permissionlessly create Reward Vaults, which then need to be whitelisted via governance to receive emissions. (doc_24, doc_25)
